front: Your server has 32 GB RAM but needs to sort 25 TB of logs by timestamp. What algorithm do you use?
back: External merge sort partitions data too large for RAM into sorted runs on disk, then merges them via a k-way min-heap. It minimizes disk I/O by reading/writing large sequential blocks since disk access is the bottleneck. Sorting 25 TB of logs on 32 GB RAM creates ~800 sorted runs sorted in-memory wi
hint: Think about what happens when the entire dataset is ~800x larger than your available memory — you can't load it all, so how do you produce a globally sorted result?
mnemonic: Picture a librarian tasked with organizing 800 unsorted bookshelves using only one small desk. She sorts each shelf individually on her desk, stacks it back, then repeatedly picks the earliest book from every shelf using a tiny appointment calendar — the k-way min-heap.
